message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  In this work we use mathematical modeling to analyse the dynamics of COVID-19 spread after a vaccination program is initiated. The model used is a delay differential equation developed earlier by our group. Basis of currently available data, our principal findings are as follows. (
                  <jats:italic>a</jats:italic>
                  ) For fastest deceleration of the pandemic, people with high interaction rate such as grocers and airline cabin crew should be given priority in vaccine access. (
                  <jats:italic>b</jats:italic>
                  ) Individuals who have been vaccinated may be selectively cleared to return to normal activities without significant risk of a resurgence in cases. (
                  <jats:italic>c</jats:italic>
                  ) If an infection as well as a vaccine confers immunity for a duration
                  <jats:italic>τ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  , then the pandemic can be eliminated by vaccinating people at a sufficiently high rate. Unless
                  <jats:italic>τ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  is very small, the cutoff rate required appears feasible to achieve in practice. (
                  <jats:italic>d</jats:italic>
                  ) The presence of a substantial minority of vaccine-hesitant population might not amount to a significant threat or even an inconvenience to a vaccine-compliant majority population.
                </jats:p>
title: COVID-19 Spreading Dynamics with Vaccination – Allocation Strategy, Return to Normalcy and Vaccine Hesitancy

reference.unstructured: B Shayak and MM Sharma , “A New approach to the dynamic modeling of an infectious disease,” submitted (2020) available at https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.10.30.20223305v1

B Shayak , MM Sharma , M Gaur and AK Mishra , “Impact of reproduction number on multiple wave spreading dynamics of COVID-19 with temporary immunity – a mathematical modeling study,” submitted (2020) available at www.shayak.in/Shayakpapers/NLD/Corona51.pdf

B Shayak and MM Sharma , “Retarded logistic equation as a universal dynamic model for the spread of COVID-19,” MedRxiv Article (2020) available at https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.06.09.20126573v1
